mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS path lowers the market discount rate on equities and supports valuation expansion, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive on average during periods of falling FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST above a recent baseline signals housing and construction demand strengthening, which leads broad cyclical earnings expectations and pushes the S&P 500 higher over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce negative or flat 60-day forward returns more often than positive ones, or if the trigger loses directional accuracy in two consecutive rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers fail to precede negative 20-day returns, or if the sign hit rate stays below 55% across a full business-cycle sample.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS and RSAFS both accelerate together, payroll growth supports incomes while retail sales confirm spending, producing a demand impulse that raises S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive condition does not outperform either proxy alone, or if positive joint readings are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO with stable labor conditions signals stronger real output and industrial utilization, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.4% month-over-month while UNRATE is unchanged or lower
failure_condition: Reject if output upturns with stable unemployment do not lead to positive 5-day returns, or if the interaction adds no incremental predictive power over INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, so higher policy rates predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 bps over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if rate-hike windows are not followed by below-baseline 20-day returns, or if the sign reverses during multiple tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while PAYEMS growth weakens, inflation squeezes real wages and margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation > 4% and PAYEMS growth is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/weak-payroll regimes do not precede negative 60-day returns, or if returns are positive in most triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS marks an improving labor market that supports consumer spending and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points in 2 months and PAYEMS rises over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if joint labor improvement does not correspond to positive 60-day returns, or if the joint signal underperforms a naive market baseline over any 3-year rolling window.
